pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: CMRX surged 476.5% over 60 days on a narrative of being 'one of the key companies' developing dopamine-receptor-antagonist drugs. Narrative Agent classified this as VAGUE: lacks specificity (no program name, stage, timeline, partner), lacks primary-source corroboration (single T3 GlobeNewswire market-analysis article), and lacks sector-expected evidence (no FDA filings, no clinical trial registry, no company press release). Alt-Data Verification Agent classified as WEAKLY_CONTRADICTED: structural absence of hard-to-fake signals (SEC filings unavailable via cik_not_found, clinical trial registry not wired, FDA filing index not wired, patent activity not wired). Surge-Short Agent recommends SHORT: the absence of regulatory/clinical footprint relative to the company's claimed involvement is itself a bear-thesis signal per sector-aware reasoning. Fundamental snapshot shows Q3 2024 revenue of only $26k with -$22.9M net loss and -$20.5M operating cash flow—inconsistent with active drug development. Analyst sentiment unchanged despite 476% surge (1 SB, 4 B, 4 H as of 2025-03-01), suggesting market skepticism. Macro regime is Overheat/Moderately Restrictive (stress_score=2), disfavoring speculative biotech. Risk Agent confirms no hard-veto conditions tripped. Preponderance of evidence supports bear thesis: momentum-driven narrative capture without real operational/regulatory catalysts. SHORT at 0.5% initial position per §4.8 fixed entry sizing.
invalidation_conditions: If CMRX files an 8-K within 48 hours of decision_timestamp disclosing a genuine partnership or IND filing for dopamine-antagonist program, bear thesis would be invalidated.
If ClinicalTrials.gov registry shows active CMRX-sponsored dopamine-antagonist trial enrollment, structural-absence signal would be contradicted.
If FDA CBER/CDER public database confirms active IND or NDA for CMRX dopamine-antagonist candidate, regulatory footprint would be established.
If institutional ownership data (13F) shows material new positions by recognized biotech investors post-surge, smart-money support would be evidenced.
If company press release from official IR channel corroborates the GlobeNewswire narrative with specific program details, primary-source corroboration would be achieved.

Extreme valuation distortion: PE -8.67, net margin -41691.98%, EV/EBITDA -7.99 (all negative due to losses).
Structural absence of regulatory footprint for claimed biotech program (no 8-K, no clinical trial registry, no FDA filings).
Single low-tier news source (GlobeNewswire market analysis, not primary disclosure).
Macro headwind: Overheat regime with strict equity discipline; biotech sector under pressure.
Analyst sentiment unchanged despite 476% surge (no upgrade or new coverage), suggesting market skepticism.
Extreme cash burn: -$20.5M operating cash flow in Q3 2024 on $26k revenue; runway concern.
Narrative-price gap is HIGH: 476% surge not matched by visible operational or regulatory catalysts in alt-data record.

Implement clinical trial registry adapter (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov) to cross-check company claims against public trial enrollment data.
Compute NDI with ≥2 sources; when NDI insufficient, flag as 'contested_narrative' rather than proceeding with single-source analysis.

pm_decision_extract.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The surge_short sleeve relies on structural-absence reasoning (Pass 6, §13.6.SOFT_REASONING). This packet demonstrates the pattern: a listed biotech company surging on a public narrative (dopamine-antagonist pipeline) should have verifiable SEC filings, clinical trial registry entries, and FDA regulatory footprint. The absence of all three, combined with deteriorating fundamentals and low-tier news sourcing, is itself the bear-thesis signal. The system correctly identifies this absence pattern without requiring external knowledge of post-decision events.
recommended_followups: Obtain CMRX 10-K (most recent) from SEC EDGAR to verify business description and pipeline claims.
Query ClinicalTrials.gov API for any CMRX-sponsored dopamine-antagonist trials.
Query FDA CBER/CDER public databases for CMRX IND or NDA filings.
Monitor SEC EDGAR for 8-K filing within 48 hours of 2025-03-05 09:30 ET.
Obtain CMRX investor relations press release archive (corporate website) for official announcement.
Calculate cash runway: total assets $158.7M, quarterly burn -$20.5M → ~7.7 quarters remaining.
Monitor insider Form 4 filings post-surge for executive selling (currently unavailable).
pm_decision_extract.reason: Structural absence of SEC filings, clinical trial registry, and FDA regulatory footprint contradicts public narrative of active dopamine-antagonist drug development. Fundamental deterioration (Q3 2024: $26k revenue, -$22.9M net loss, -$20.5M operating cash flow) inconsistent with claimed pipeline activity. Single low-tier news source (GlobeNewswire market analysis, not primary disclosure). Analyst sentiment unchanged despite 476% surge. Macro regime (Overheat/Moderately Restrictive) disfavors speculative biotech. Preponderance of evidence supports bear thesis: momentum-driven narrative capture, not real value creation.

rule_engine_path: candidate_type=surge_short → evaluate surge-short entry framework per §2.9–§2.12 and §4.8
Pre-filter checks: prior_close=5.04, trigger_day_open=8.4 → gap=66.7% ≥50% ✓; volume=2.03M ≥1M ✓; prior_close=5.04 >$2 ✓
Never-short filters (§2.9–§2.12): no reverse-split history, no delisting, no bankruptcy, no regulatory suspension detected
Bear-thesis evaluation (Pass 7.1 preponderance-of-evidence):
(i) Surge dynamic exhaustion: price surged 476% over 60d, gapped 66.7% on trigger day; no intraday crash or volume inversion visible in packet; dynamic appears intact but momentum-driven

(ii) Narrative inconsistency: public narrative claims 'key company in dopamine-antagonist market'; sector-aware reasoning expects SEC filings, clinical trial registry, FDA regulatory footprint; all absent

(iii) Structural absence: sec_edgar cik_not_found (no 8-K), biotech_clinical_adapter not wired (no trial registry), FDA filing index not wired (no regulatory footprint); absence relative to sector-expected evidence is bear-thesis input per §5.17
(iv) Fundamental ratios: revenue $26k, net loss -$22.9M, operating cash flow -$20.5M, PE -8.67, net margin -41691.98%; negative/zero ratios combined with extreme price action support short
(v) Corporate action history: no reverse-split or pump-related history detected in packet

Preponderance assessment: dimensions (ii), (iii), (iv) point strongly toward bear thesis; dimension (i) is neutral (surge intact but momentum-driven); dimension (v) is neutral (no history). Coherence of bear thesis is HIGH: narrative-vs-evidence gap + structural absence + fundamental deterioration + macro headwind form integrated case for short.
Risk veto check: no hard-veto conditions tripped per Risk Agent
Position sizing: §4.8 fixed entry sizing for NEW surge_short = 0.005 (0.5%)
Execution plan: T+1 next-open per §4.8 and decision_time_discipline
Final decision: SHORT at 0.005 position_size_pct

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: CMRX is a biotech company (inferred from sector context: dopamine receptor antagonist therapeutics). The single news item (published 2025-03-04 18:00 UTC, before cutoff) is a GlobeNewswire / DelveInsight industry analysis article titled 'Dopamine Receptor Antagonists Clinical Trial Pipeline Analysis: 10+ Key Companies Shaping the Future of Dopamine Receptor Antagonists Therapeutics.' The article mentions CMRX as 'one of the key companies' in the dopamine receptor antagonist market and frames the market as 'poised for significant growth.' SECTOR-AWARE REASONING: For a biotech company claiming involvement in a growing therapeutic area, expected corroborating evidence would include: (1) FDA filings (IND, NDA, ANDA), (2) clinical trial registry entries, (3) patent/IP disclosures, (4) R&D expense trends, (5) press releases from the company itself announcing programs or milestones. STRUCTURAL ABSENCE PATTERN: The news item is a third-party industry report (T3 source per information_integrity_assessment), not a company press release or official disclosure. No SEC 8-K filing confirmation (adapter failed: cik_not_found). No biotech_clinical_adapter data (not_connected). No company-authored press release in news feed. The article contains NO specific dollar amounts, NO named customer/partner, NO clinical trial enrollment numbers, NO FDA filing dates, NO program names beyond the generic 'dopamine receptor antagonist' category. The claim 'Chimerix is one of the key companies' is vague and unsupported by concrete evidence in the packet. Wikipedia pageviews for 'RTCM_SC-104' (an unrelated technical standard, not a drug program) show modest activity (4–34 views/day over Feb–Mar 2025), with no spike coinciding with the news publication. Price action: +6.21% (5d), +30.53% (20d), +476.54% (60d) — substantial move, but no catalyst-specific corroboration. Fundamental snapshot (Q3 2024): revenue $26k, net income -$22.9M, gross margin 100% (likely pre-revenue or near-zero revenue), net margin -87,996% — company is deeply unprofitable and pre-commercial. The article's framing of 'market growth' and 'novel therapeutics' is generic industry commentary, not a concrete catalyst tied to CMRX's pipeline. No earnings call, no investor presentation, no clinical data release mentioned. VERDICT: The narrative is VAGUE. It lacks specificity (no program name, no stage, no timeline, no partner), lacks primary-source corroboration (third-party report only), and lacks sector-expected evidence (no FDA filings, no clinical trial registry, no company press release). The absence of expected biotech-sector evidence relative to the company's claimed involvement is itself a bear-thesis signal per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING and SECTOR-AWARE REASONING SEQUENCE.
invalidation_conditions: If a company press release or SEC 8-K filing emerges post-cutoff announcing a specific clinical program, partnership, or FDA milestone, the vague-narrative verdict would be invalidated.
If biotech_clinical_adapter data becomes available and shows active IND or NDA filings for CMRX dopamine antagonist programs, the absence-as-signal pattern would be contradicted.
If Wikipedia pageviews for CMRX-specific drug program pages (not RTCM_SC-104) show abnormal spikes coinciding with the news publication, attention corroboration would strengthen the catalyst.
If analyst reports or institutional research notes cite specific CMRX programs with clinical timelines, the vague-narrative verdict could shift to 'supported'.

alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: CMRX is a biotech/pharma company. The single news item (2025-03-04, GlobeNewswire) claims CMRX is 'one of the key companies' in dopamine receptor antagonist therapeutics, with positive sentiment. However, this narrative lacks hard-to-fake corroboration. Sector-expected alt-data (clinical trial registry, FDA filings, patent activity, SEC filings) is either unavailable (adapters not wired) or failed to return rows (sec_edgar cik_not_found, sec_form4 cik_not_found). Wikipedia pageviews show modest, volatile attention (4–34 views/day on RTCM_SC-104 article, peaking 2025-02-28 at 34 views, declining to 25 by 2025-03-05). This is not abnormal for a micro-cap biotech. Analyst sentiment (FMP) shows 1 Strong Buy, 4 Buy, 4 Hold as of 2025-03-01 — supportive but descriptor-only (T2 tier, not primary source). Fundamental snapshot (Q3 2024) shows revenue of $26k, net loss of $22.9M, negative margins, minimal cash burn. Valuation is distressed (PE=-8.67, PB=6.30, EV/EBITDA=-7.99). Price has surged 476% in 60 days (from ~$0.90 to $4.96), with 30% gain in 20 days. The macro regime is 'Overheat / Moderately Restrictive' (stress_score=2), which caps equity allocation and demands strict quality/valuation caution. The narrative ('key company in growing market') is plausible for a biotech but is NOT corroborated by hard-to-fake evidence. The absence of SEC filings (cik_not_found) is a structural red flag for a public company. The absence of clinical trial registry or FDA filing data (adapters not wired) is a sector-expected gap that cannot be filled. Wikipedia attention is modest and declining. The news item is a single T3 source (GlobeNewswire, industry analysis reprint) with no independent confirmation. Analyst ratings are supportive but are descriptor-only and do not constitute primary corroboration. Conclusion: The narrative is WEAKLY CONTRADICTED by structural absence of hard-to-fake evidence relative to what a legitimate biotech company should produce (SEC filings, clinical trial data, patent activity). The surge in price (476% in 60d) is not matched by visible operational or regulatory catalysts in the alt-data record.
invalidation_conditions: If sec_edgar adapter returns rows showing recent 8-K or 10-Q filings with clinical trial updates or regulatory approvals, narrative support would increase.
If biotech_clinical_adapter (when wired) returns active trial enrollment or Phase advancement data, narrative would be corroborated.
If FDA filing index shows recent IND or NDA submissions, narrative would be supported.
If patent activity shows recent dopamine antagonist patent filings, narrative would be partially corroborated.
If Wikipedia pageviews spike sharply (>100 views/day sustained) coinciding with news, attention would be abnormal and warrant caution.

alt_data_verify.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet lacks sector-specific adapters (biotech_clinical_adapter, FDA filing index) that are essential for pharma verification. The sec_edgar cik_not_found error is a critical blocker that prevents verification of company legitimacy and recent disclosures. The information_integrity_assessment block is a placeholder in v1 with pollution-defense layer unimplemented; this limits ability to assess coordinated campaign risk or low-tier-only narrative pollution. The alternative_data_features block shows wikipedia_pageviews as the only wired adapter with data; all industry-specific adapters are not_connected. This creates a structural gap in alt-data corroboration for a biotech candidate.
recommended_followups: Verify CMRX CIK and SEC filing status; if delisted or non-reporting, narrative credibility drops sharply.
Retrieve clinical trial registry data manually (ClinicalTrials.gov) to assess trial status and enrollment.
Check FDA Orange Book and patent databases for dopamine antagonist IP owned by CMRX.
Cross-check analyst ratings (FMP) against recent research reports to assess rating recency and conviction.
Monitor Wikipedia pageviews and news velocity for abnormal spikes that might indicate coordinated campaign.
alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: CMRX has surged 476% in 60 days (from ~$0.90 to $4.96) on a narrative of being 'one of the key companies' in dopamine receptor antagonist therapeutics. However, hard-to-fake alt-data corroboration is absent: SEC filings unavailable (cik_not_found), clinical trial registry not wired, FDA filing index not wired, patent activity not wired. Wikipedia pageviews are modest and declining (25 views on decision date). Analyst sentiment is supportive (1 SB, 4 B, 4 H) but descriptor-only. Single T3 news source (GlobeNewswire reprint) without primary disclosure. Fundamental snapshot shows severe losses ($22.9M net loss on $26k revenue) and distressed valuation (PE=-8.67, PB=6.30). Macro regime is restrictive (stress_score=2), capping equity allocation at 20% and demanding strict quality/valuation caution. The price surge is NOT matched by visible operational or regulatory catalysts in the alt-data record. Narrative-price gap is HIGH.
alt_data_verify.adapter_selection_reason: wikipedia_pageviews is the only alternative-data adapter with rows returned (30 rows, 2025-02-04 to 2025-03-05). Sector-specific adapters (biotech_clinical_adapter, FDA filing index, patent activity) are not wired. Default fallback to wikipedia_pageviews per adapter_selection.fallback_to_default_adapter=true.
alt_data_verify.hard_to_fake_signal_summary: No hard-to-fake signals present. SEC filings (cik_not_found), clinical trial registry (not wired), FDA filings (not wired), and patent activity (not wired) are all unavailable or failed. Wikipedia pageviews show modest, declining attention (25 views on decision date, down from 34 peak). Analyst sentiment (FMP) is supportive (1 SB, 4 B, 4 H) but is descriptor-only (T2 tier, not primary source). News item is single T3 source (GlobeNewswire reprint) without independent confirmation. Fundamental snapshot shows severe losses and negative profitability. No hard-to-fake corroboration of the 'key company in growing market' narrative.

surge_short.reasoning_summary: CMRX surged 476.5% over 60 days and gapped 66.7% on 2025-03-05 open. The public narrative (single GlobeNewswire article, 2025-03-04) claims CMRX is 'one of the key companies' developing dopamine-receptor-antagonist drugs. However, structural-absence evidence contradicts this claim: (1) No SEC 8-K filing exists confirming the announcement or partnership (sec_edgar adapter returned cik_not_found). (2) No clinical trial registry entries are present in the packet for CMRX dopamine-antagonist programs. (3) Fundamental data shows Q3 2024 revenue of only $26k with -$22.9M net loss and -$20.5M operating cash flow—inconsistent with a company claiming active drug development. (4) The single news item is a low-tier (T3/T4) market-analysis article, not a primary-source disclosure from CMRX. (5) Wikipedia pageviews spike on 'RTCM_SC-104' (a technical standard unrelated to the company). (6) Analyst sentiment unchanged (1 SB, 4 B, 4 H as of 2025-03-01), suggesting no material update to consensus. (7) Macro regime is Overheat/Moderately Restrictive, disfavoring speculative biotech. The surge appears to be momentum-driven narrative capture rather than real value creation. For a biotech company claiming active drug development, the absence of SEC filings, clinical trial registry entries, and FDA regulatory footprint is a strong bear-thesis signal under the sector-aware reasoning framework (Pass 6).
invalidation_conditions: If CMRX files an 8-K within 48 hours of decision_timestamp disclosing a genuine partnership or IND filing for dopamine-antagonist program
If ClinicalTrials.gov registry shows active CMRX-sponsored dopamine-antagonist trial enrollment
If FDA CBER/CDER public database confirms active IND or NDA for CMRX dopamine-antagonist candidate
If institutional ownership data (13F) shows material new positions by recognized biotech investors post-surge
If company press release from official IR channel corroborates the GlobeNewswire narrative with specific program details

surge_short.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The surge_short sleeve relies on structural-absence reasoning (Pass 6, §13.6.SOFT_REASONING). This packet demonstrates the pattern: a listed biotech company surging on a public narrative (dopamine-antagonist pipeline) should have verifiable SEC filings, clinical trial registry entries, and FDA regulatory footprint. The absence of all three, combined with deteriorating fundamentals and low-tier news sourcing, is itself the bear-thesis signal. The system correctly identifies this absence pattern without requiring external knowledge of post-decision events.
recommended_followups: Obtain CMRX 10-K (most recent) from SEC EDGAR to verify business description and pipeline claims
Query ClinicalTrials.gov API for any CMRX-sponsored dopamine-antagonist trials
Query FDA CBER/CDER public databases for CMRX IND or NDA filings
Monitor SEC EDGAR for 8-K filing within 48 hours of 2025-03-05 09:30 ET
Obtain CMRX investor relations press release archive (corporate website) for official announcement
Calculate cash runway: total assets $158.7M, quarterly burn -$20.5M → ~7.7 quarters remaining
Monitor insider Form 4 filings post-surge for executive selling (currently unavailable)

pm.reasoning_summary: CMRX surged 476.5% over 60 days on a narrative of being 'one of the key companies' developing dopamine-receptor-antagonist drugs. Narrative Agent classified this as VAGUE: lacks specificity (no program name, stage, timeline, partner), lacks primary-source corroboration (single T3 GlobeNewswire market-analysis article), and lacks sector-expected evidence (no FDA filings, no clinical trial registry, no company press release). Alt-Data Verification Agent classified as WEAKLY_CONTRADICTED: structural absence of hard-to-fake signals (SEC filings unavailable via cik_not_found, clinical trial registry not wired, FDA filing index not wired, patent activity not wired). Surge-Short Agent recommends SHORT: the absence of regulatory/clinical footprint relative to the company's claimed involvement is itself a bear-thesis signal per sector-aware reasoning. Fundamental snapshot shows Q3 2024 revenue of only $26k with -$22.9M net loss and -$20.5M operating cash flow—inconsistent with active drug development. Analyst sentiment unchanged despite 476% surge (1 SB, 4 B, 4 H as of 2025-03-01), suggesting market skepticism. Macro regime is Overheat/Moderately Restrictive (stress_score=2), disfavoring speculative biotech. Risk Agent confirms no hard-veto conditions tripped. Preponderance of evidence supports bear thesis: momentum-driven narrative capture without real operational/regulatory catalysts. SHORT at 0.5% initial position per §4.8 fixed entry sizing.
invalidation_conditions: If CMRX files an 8-K within 48 hours of decision_timestamp disclosing a genuine partnership or IND filing for dopamine-antagonist program, bear thesis would be invalidated.
If ClinicalTrials.gov registry shows active CMRX-sponsored dopamine-antagonist trial enrollment, structural-absence signal would be contradicted.
If FDA CBER/CDER public database confirms active IND or NDA for CMRX dopamine-antagonist candidate, regulatory footprint would be established.
If institutional ownership data (13F) shows material new positions by recognized biotech investors post-surge, smart-money support would be evidenced.
If company press release from official IR channel corroborates the GlobeNewswire narrative with specific program details, primary-source corroboration would be achieved.

rule_engine_path: candidate_type=surge_short → evaluate surge-short entry framework per §2.9–§2.12 and §4.8

Never-short filters (§2.9–§2.12): no reverse-split history, no delisting, no bankruptcy, no regulatory suspension detected
Bear-thesis evaluation (Pass 7.1 preponderance-of-evidence):
(i) Surge dynamic exhaustion: price surged 476% over 60d, gapped 66.7% on trigger day; no intraday crash or volume inversion visible in packet; dynamic appears intact but momentum-driven

(ii) Narrative inconsistency: public narrative claims 'key company in dopamine-antagonist market'; sector-aware reasoning expects SEC filings, clinical trial registry, FDA regulatory footprint; all absent

(iii) Structural absence: sec_edgar cik_not_found (no 8-K), biotech_clinical_adapter not wired (no trial registry), FDA filing index not wired (no regulatory footprint); absence relative to sector-expected evidence is bear-thesis input per §5.17
(iv) Fundamental ratios: revenue $26k, net loss -$22.9M, operating cash flow -$20.5M, PE -8.67, net margin -41691.98%; negative/zero ratios combined with extreme price action support short
(v) Corporate action history: no reverse-split or pump-related history detected in packet

Preponderance assessment: dimensions (ii), (iii), (iv) point strongly toward bear thesis; dimension (i) is neutral (surge intact but momentum-driven); dimension (v) is neutral (no history). Coherence of bear thesis is HIGH: narrative-vs-evidence gap + structural absence + fundamental deterioration + macro headwind form integrated case for short.
Risk veto check: no hard-veto conditions tripped per Risk Agent
Position sizing: §4.8 fixed entry sizing for NEW surge_short = 0.005 (0.5%)
Execution plan: T+1 next-open per §4.8 and decision_time_discipline
Final decision: SHORT at 0.005 position_size_pct

pm.unverified_claim_note: Public narrative (GlobeNewswire article) claims CMRX is 'one of the key companies' developing dopamine-receptor-antagonist drugs. Claim is unverified: no SEC 8-K filing, no clinical trial registry entries, no FDA regulatory footprint, no company press release. Structural absence of hard-to-fake evidence relative to sector-expected footprint is bear-thesis signal per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception.
pm.thesis_confirmation_block_reason: Surge-short thesis does not require fundamental confirmation per §2.11. Bear thesis is supported by structural-absence reasoning (Pass 6, §13.6.SOFT_REASONING): narrative claims active drug development but lacks verifiable SEC filings, clinical trial registry, and FDA regulatory footprint. Absence relative to sector-expected evidence is itself the signal.
